SPOTTER MEASURES WIND GUST TO 62 MPH. 1/2 INCH HAIL WITH THUNDERSTORM. 1.03 INCHES OF RAINFALL AT THE TIME OF OBSERVATION.
SPOTTER REPORTS WATER RUNNING HALFWAY UP TO HOUSES AND LOTS OF STANDING WATER. WAS ABLE TO DRIVE HOME ALONG RURAL ROADWAYS.
EL PASO POLICE DEPT REPORTS LARGE SINK HOLE DUE TO EXCESSIVE RAINFALL ON I-10W. CLOSURES DUE TO FLOODING ON BORDER EXPRESSWAY AND OTHER ROUTES CAUSING MAJOR TRAFFIC IMPACTS.
WATER STARTED ENTERING HOMES AROUND 4 PM AS NEARBY DRAINAGE POND OVERFLOWED.

*** 1 FATAL *** 39 YEAR OLD FEMALE HIKER SWEPT AWAY BY RAINWATER WITHIN THE THUNDERBIRD TRAIL IN WEST EL PASO. CONFIRMED FATALITY BY LAW ENFORCEMENT. NEAREST RAIN GAUGE HAS RECORDED OVER 4 INCHES OF RAIN IN THE PAST 36 HOURS.
FLOODING REPORTED BY MESA ST AND BRENTWOOD AVE.
FLOODING ACROSS I-10, AT LEAST 3 LANES WERE CLOSED. PAISANO DR AT EXECUTIVE BLVD HAS BEEN CLOSED TOO DUE TO FLOODING TOO.
TXDOT CLOSED DONIPHAN ROAD AT CANUTILLO DUE TO FLOODING.
FLOODING AT MESA AND MONTECILLO BLVD. POLICE STOPPED TRAFFIC DRIVING NORTH ALONG MESA STREET.
A CURRENT OF WATER WAS FLOWING OVER CAMELIA RD IN CANUTILLO.
VIDEO OBTAINED FROM SOCIAL MEDIA OF A HOME WITH SEVERAL INCHES OF WATER INSIDE AND DAMAGE TO POSSESSIONS EVIDENT.
GATEWAY SOUTH NEAR FRED WILSON IS IMPASSABLE DUE TO FLOODING. A CAR IS STRANDED IN THE FLOOD WATERS. US-54 AT ELLERTHORPE ALSO HAS WATER ACROSS THE ROADWAY RESULTING IN CLOSURES.
HEAVY RAIN PROVOKED A ROCKSLIDE THAT BLOCKED MCKELLIGON CANYON RD. ABOUT 100 PEOPLE WERE STUCK AT COOL CANYON NIGHTS WHILE A BULLDOZER CLEARED THE ROAD FROM THE DEBRIS.
SPOTTER RELAYED INFORMATION FROM FIRE DEPT DISPATCH THAT HWY 130 IS BEING CLOSED DUE TO FLOODING.
MESCALERO TRIBE EM REPORTS FLASH FLOODING IS ONGOING ALONG MUCH OF US-70 NEAR MILE MARKER 250 AND NM-244 NORTHEAST OF MESCALERO. TORRENTIAL RAINFALL OCCURRED OVER THE PAST HOUR WITH ZERO VISIBILITY.

*** 1 FATAL, 1 INJ *** FLASH FLOODING CAUSED A WALL COLLAPSE AT A LOCAL RESIDENCE, REQUIRING FIRE RESCUE. A 65 YEAR OLD WOMAN DIED AS A RESULT AND A 2 YEAR OLD GIRL WAS HOSPITALIZED IN CRITICAL CONDITION.
PUBLIC REPORT ON SOCIAL MEDIA, FLASH FLOODING (08/12/21) AT ATLAS AVE CAUSED THE ROAD TO BUCKLE WITH SLABS OF PAVEMENT MISSING.
GARAGE WITH A FEW INCHES OF STANDING WATER, ALONG WITH A LOT OF FLOODING AROUND THE LA UNION AND CANUTILLO AREA.
FLASH FLOODING AT THE INTERSECTION OF FORT BLVD AND LOUISIANA ST. A TORRENT OF WATER RUSHING DOWN THE MOUNTAINSIDE INTO NEIGHBORHOODS.
WATER TOPPED EARTHEN DAM AND RESULTED IN FLOODING OF LA UNION. SEVERAL CARS WASHED OFF THE ROAD.
ELEVATED AREA IN ANTHONY THAT DRAINS EASILY HAD 2 INCHES OF STANDING WATER IN THEIR YARD.
